description: Figure 7 presents Mott-Schottky curves plotting the reciprocal of squared capacitance (C^{-2}) versus applied potential (V_{SCE}) for Fe-30Mn-8Al-C-xCu low-density steels with Cu contents of 0, 1, and 3 wt%. The graph is divided into three distinct potential regions: Region I (approximately -1.0 to -0.5 V_{SCE}) exhibiting n-type semiconductor characteristics with decreasing C^{-2} as potential increases; Region II (approximately -0.5 to -0.3 V_{SCE}) representing the depletion zone where C^{-2} reaches maximum values; and Region III (> -0.3 V_{SCE}) where C^{-2} approaches zero. The curves demonstrate that increasing Cu content systematically reduces C^{-2} values across all potential regions. The caption and associated data indicate that donor densities (N_D) increase from 0.81×10^{18} cm^{-3} (0Cu) to 1.37×10^{18} cm^{-3} (1Cu) and 1.45×10^{18} cm^{-3} (3Cu), while acceptor densities (N_A) rise from 2.62×10^{18} cm^{-3} to 3.40×10^{18} cm^{-3} and 6.07×10^{18} cm^{-3}, respectively. This trend confirms that Cu alloying increases charge carrier concentrations in the passive film, correlating with enhanced susceptibility to pitting corrosion in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution.
